message:
fix(ifc): correct NeverSink semantics to allow Public data and add Clearance::can_receive_label()

NeverSink was documented and implemented as "cannot receive any labeled data"
but this is semantically wrong — Public data (level 0) should always be
receivable without declassification. Corrects the invariant:

ifc_artifacts.rs:
- NeverSink::max_receivable_label_level() returns Some(0) instead of None,
  allowing Public-level data through while still blocking Internal and above
- Update ClearanceClass docs and tests to reflect "can receive only Public"

flow_lattice.rs:
- Add Clearance::can_receive_label(&self, label) convenience method that
  compares label.level() against max_label_level()
- Refine apply_declassification() to use can_receive_label() for the sink
  clearance check instead of the less precise LabelClass::can_flow_to(),
  which operated on label classes rather than actual clearance levels
- Add enrichment_custom_sink_label_above_open_sink_rejected test ensuring
  Custom labels with level > max are properly rejected
- Update error message: "declassified label" → "sink clearance" for clarity

frankenctl.rs: let-chain refactoring in write_bytes_file() (if-let + &&)

lib.rs: register new asupersync_contract_matrix public module

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
